1.11: To this end we always pray for you, that our God may count you worthy of his calling and may fulfill every resolve for goodness and every work of faith with power,
1.3: We are bound to give thanks to God always for you, brothers, as is fitting, because your faith is growing more and more, and the love of each one of you for all the others is increasing.
1.7: and to give relief to you who are afflicted along with us, when the Lord Jesus is revealed from heaven with his mighty angels,
1.10: when he comes to be glorified in his saints and to be marveled at among all who have believed—because our testimony to you was believed—on that day.
1.9: These will pay the penalty of eternal destruction, away from the presence of the Lord and from the glory of his strength,
1.4: Therefore we ourselves boast about you among the churches of God for your endurance and faith in all your persecutions and afflictions that you are enduring.
1.6: Since indeed it is just on God's part to repay with affliction those who afflict you,
1.2: Grace to you and peace from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
1.12: so that the name of our Lord Jesus may be glorified in you, and you in him, according to the grace of our God and the Lord Jesus Christ.
1.5: This is evidence of the righteous judgment of God, so that you may be considered worthy of the kingdom of God, for which you are also suffering.
1.8: in flaming fire, inflicting vengeance on those who do not know God and on those who do not obey the gospel of our Lord Jesus.
1.1: Paul and Silvanus and Timothy, to the church of the Thessalonians in God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ:

2.16: Now may our Lord Jesus Christ himself, and God our Father, who loved us and gave us eternal encouragement and good hope through grace,
2.17: comfort your hearts and strengthen you in every good work and word.
2.13: But we are bound to give thanks to God always for you, brothers and sisters beloved by the Lord, because God chose you as the firstfruits for salvation through sanctification by the Spirit and belief in the truth.
2.10: and with all the deception of wickedness for those who are perishing, because they did not accept the love of the truth so as to be saved.
2.1: Now we ask you, brothers, with regard to the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ and our gathering together to him,
2.15: So then, brothers, stand firm and hold to the traditions that you were taught—whether by our word or by our letter.
2.12: so that all who did not believe the truth but delighted in wickedness may be judged.
2.3: Let no one deceive you in any way, for it will not come unless the rebellion comes first, and the man of lawlessness is revealed—the son of destruction,
2.8: And then the lawless one will be revealed, whom the Lord Jesus will slay with the breath of his mouth and bring to nothing by the brightness of his coming.
2.7: For the mystery of lawlessness is already at work; only the one who now restrains will do so until he is out of the way.
2.4: who opposes and exalts himself above every so-called god or object of worship, so that he takes his seat in the temple of God, proclaiming himself to be God.
2.2: that you not be quickly shaken in mind or be alarmed, either by a spirit or by a spoken word or by a letter supposedly from us, to the effect that the day of the Lord has come.
2.6: And you know what is restraining him now, so that he will be revealed in his time.
2.11: And for this reason God sends them a powerful delusion so that they will believe the lie,
2.14: To this end he also called you through our gospel, so that you may obtain the glory of our Lord Jesus Christ.
2.9: The coming of the lawless one is by the activity of Satan with all power and false signs and wonders,
2.5: Do you not remember that when I was still with you I was telling you these things?

3.5: May the Lord direct your hearts to the love of God and to the steadfastness of Christ.
3.15: Yet do not regard him as an enemy, but warn him as a brother.
3.1: Finally, brothers, pray for us that the word of the Lord may spread rapidly and be honored, as it was with you,
3.13: But as for you, brothers, do not grow weary in doing good.
3.6: Now we command you, brothers, in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, to keep away from any brother living in idleness and not according to the tradition that he received from us.
3.18: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all.
3.11: For we hear that some among you are living in idleness, not busy at work, but busybodies.
3.8: nor did we eat anyone's food without payment, but with labor and hardship, working night and day so as not to be a burden to any of you;
3.9: not because we do not have the right, but in order to give you an example to imitate.
3.2: and that we may be delivered from unreasonable and evil men, for not all have faith.
3.3: But the Lord is faithful, who will strengthen you and guard you against the evil one.
3.12: Now such persons we command and encourage in the Lord Jesus Christ to work in quietness and to eat their own food.
3.17: I, Paul, write this greeting with my own hand. This is the sign in every letter; this is how I write.
3.7: For you yourselves know how you ought to imitate us, for we were not idle among you,
3.14: But if anyone does not obey what we say in this letter, take note of that person and do not associate with him, so that he may be put to shame.
3.16: Now may the Lord of peace himself give you peace at all times in every way. The Lord be with you all.
3.10: For even when we were with you, we were giving you this command: If anyone is not willing to work, let him not eat.
3.4: And we have confidence in the Lord concerning you, that you are doing and will continue to do the things that we command.
